content.full_text:
State of California   Lobbyist Contribution to Officials                 862202

Memorandum   Spicer - Reputable


To  : Opinion request meeting participants.              Date : Feb. 10, 1977


                                                         711-77-528

From  : FAIR POLITICAL PRACTICES COMMISSION
        Natalie West

Subject: Item for discussion.

        Marty Pike, of Winner, Wagner and Associates, has asked
        whether a lobbyist may make a campaign contribution to a
        candidate for local school board. The candidate is the
        spouse of a State Energy Commissioner and the lobbyist
        lobbies the Energy Commission.

        It is our tentative conclusion that the contribution is
        permissible under the standard set forth in the Cory
        opinion, 2 PPC Opinions 43. Should the lobbyist report
        the contribution as a "payment to influence . . ." (See
        Section 82045(c)), pursuant to Section 86107(g). Can
        additional information be required pursuant to Section
        86107(g) without adopting a regulation?

NW:jo    A lobbyist may contribute to the
         campaign of an official's spouse but the
         contribution is reportable as a payment to
         influence (cletify expense) pursuant to
         18 615(b)(1)(a). Clarity expense is
         defined at    18615(a)(2)

embedding.qa_text: **Memorandum** **To:** Opinion request meeting participants. **Date:** Feb. 10, 1977 **From:** FAIR POLITICAL PRACTICES COMMISSION Natalie West **Subject:** Item for discussion. Marty Pike, of Winner, Wagner and Associates, has asked whether a lobbyist may make a campaign contribution to a candidate for local school board. The candidate is the spouse of a State Energy Commissioner and the lobbyist lobbies the Energy Commission. It is our tentative conclusion that the contribution is permissible under the standard set forth in the Cory opinion, 2 FPPC Opinions 43. Should the lobbyist report the contribution as a "payment to influence ..." (See Section 82045(c)), pursuant to Section 86107(g). Can additional information be required pursuant to Section 86107(g) without adopting a regulation? NW:jo A lobbyist may contribute to the campaign of an official's spouse but the contribution is reportable as a payment to influence (activity expense) pursuant to 18615(b)(1)(a). Activity expense is defined at 18615(a)(2).
